results.body: Taste receptors are more than sensory “buds” on the tongue — they are widely distributed chemoreceptors found throughout the body, particularly on mucosal surfaces such as the gastrointestinal, respiratory, and genitourinary tracts, as well as in organs like the heart, liver, ovaries, and testes.
